verses.text: In old age David still trusts the LORD, asking Him to be 'my strong habitation, whereunto I may continually resort.' His refuge is unchanged by years. The God who sheltered our youth is the same stronghold for our age.

In old age David still trusts the LORD, asking Him to be 'my strong habitation, whereunto I may continually resort.' His refuge is unchanged by years. The God who sheltered our youth is the same stronghold for our age.

In old age David still trusts the LORD, asking Him to be 'my strong habitation, whereunto I may continually resort.' His refuge is unchanged by years. The God who sheltered our youth is the same stronghold for our age.

He pleads deliverance from the wicked, for God has been 'my trust from my youth' and his upholder from the womb. Lifelong dependence is his plea. The God who has kept us from birth will not fail us at the last.

He pleads deliverance from the wicked, for God has been 'my trust from my youth' and his upholder from the womb. Lifelong dependence is his plea. The God who has kept us from birth will not fail us at the last.

He pleads deliverance from the wicked, for God has been 'my trust from my youth' and his upholder from the womb. Lifelong dependence is his plea. The God who has kept us from birth will not fail us at the last.
Though many marvel at his afflictions, God is his strong refuge, and his mouth is filled with praise. He turns wonder into worship. The believer answers a watching world by praising God all the day.
Though many marvel at his afflictions, God is his strong refuge, and his mouth is filled with praise. He turns wonder into worship. The believer answers a watching world by praising God all the day.
He prays, 'Cast me not off in the time of old age,' while enemies say God has forsaken him. Age has its peculiar fears. But God does not abandon the grey-headed who lean on Him.
He prays, 'Cast me not off in the time of old age,' while enemies say God has forsaken him. Age has its peculiar fears. But God does not abandon the grey-headed who lean on Him.
He prays, 'Cast me not off in the time of old age,' while enemies say God has forsaken him. Age has its peculiar fears. But God does not abandon the grey-headed who lean on Him.
He cries for God to be near and to confound his adversaries. He longs for present help. The aged saint still brings his foes and his fears to God.
He cries for God to be near and to confound his adversaries. He longs for present help. The aged saint still brings his foes and his fears to God.

'But I will hope continually,' he says, telling of God's righteousness and salvation all the day, more than he can number. Hope keeps him praising. A heart fixed on God's mercies has endless cause for song.

'But I will hope continually,' he says, telling of God's righteousness and salvation all the day, more than he can number. Hope keeps him praising. A heart fixed on God's mercies has endless cause for song.

'But I will hope continually,' he says, telling of God's righteousness and salvation all the day, more than he can number. Hope keeps him praising. A heart fixed on God's mercies has endless cause for song.

Taught of God from his youth, he begs not to be forsaken in old age until he has shown God's strength 'unto this generation.' He longs to pass on his faith. The elderly believer's great desire is to leave a testimony to those who follow.

Taught of God from his youth, he begs not to be forsaken in old age until he has shown God's strength 'unto this generation.' He longs to pass on his faith. The elderly believer's great desire is to leave a testimony to those who follow.
God's righteousness reaches the heavens; though He let David see troubles, He will revive and comfort him again. Trials are not the end. The God who brings low will lift up and console His servant.
God's righteousness reaches the heavens; though He let David see troubles, He will revive and comfort him again. Trials are not the end. The God who brings low will lift up and console His servant.
God's righteousness reaches the heavens; though He let David see troubles, He will revive and comfort him again. Trials are not the end. The God who brings low will lift up and console His servant.

He vows to praise God with harp and song, his tongue telling of God's righteousness all day, for his foes are ashamed. Deliverance overflows in music. The rescued soul cannot keep from declaring God's faithfulness.
